subject: Reg CB Counter Taobao Weekly Report 20250731
deck_filename: Counter Taobao Weekly Update - July Full Month.pdf

content.answer:
Hi Ian, Jianghong,

Please find the weekly Counter Taobao project update as of July Full Month
below.

Key Highlights


   -

   Overall:
   -

      Topline: ADO 61K (91% RR), MoM +24%, ADG $653K (89% RR), MoM +13%,
      mostly caused by Beauty underperformance.
      -

      SKU#: SKU# at 1,051K (99% RR) due to Lever 3 (ERP) delay in
      onboarding process


   -

   Fashion:
   -

      Topline: ADO 41K (97% RR), ADG $423K (90% RR), ADO% to CB 4.4% (MoM
      +0.1pp). Low RR impacted by underperformance of Lever 2 in PH,
driven by a
      decline in top sellers' performance following a scale-back in June
      over-investment to counter TTS.
      -

      SKU#: SKU# at 747k (101% RR). Lever 3 at RR 197%, showing strong ramp
      up as onboarded sellers begin listing. SKU# is expected to reach
2M in Aug
      through Lever 3 seller mass pitching
      -

      Program PC2: -1.5% driven by re-investment in program sellers from
      MY(-0.7%) TH (-1.9%) and PH (-4.9%)



   -

   Furniture:
   -

      Topline: ADO 2.1k (97% RR), ADG $96K (99% RR), ADO% to CB maintained
      at 0.6% MoM driven by underperformance in SG & TH from top seller 润泽优 due
      to operational issues (penalties, SKU delisting) and traffic
decline in 中南
      after reducing ads spending upon maxing their budget; both are
expected to
      recover next month.
      -

      SKU#: SKU# at 265k (RR at 97%) , impacted by operational issues from
      top seller 润泽优 in MY/TH—SKU delisting and upload quota limits,
recovery in
      progress, WoW +2K SKUs
      -

      Program PC2: 7.4%
      -

   F&B
   -

      Topline: ADO 1.0K (99% RR), ADG $7.9K (93% RR), ADO% to CB at 1.6%
      (MoM +0.8pp) due to limited seller pool due to restriction to grow only
      sellers dedicated to Snack, Beverages and Condiment sub-categories which
      are more long tail in nature. Next step: We will expand our seller
      select

+Jinyu Du <jinyu.du@shopee.com> +Larry Lin <larry.lin@shopee.com>

*Best regards,*

*LI Ning*
SCS

+Jinyu Du <jinyu.du@shopee.com> +Larry Lin <larry.lin@shopee.com>

*Best regards,*

*LI Ning*
SCS

+Jinyu Du <jinyu.du@shopee.com> +Larry Lin <larry.lin@shopee.com>

*Best regards,*

*LI Ning*
SCS

Hi Ian,

Please find responses in blue below

Slide 9

   -

   Performance is not very good for such a key cat?

Performance in July was below expectations mainly driven by challenges in
TH and VN, both related to top seller:

   -

   TH: Underperformance was largely due to top seller Judydoll (ADO MoM
   -13%), with a 20% drop in ad spending following an operational staff
   change. Performance is expected to recover in August as operations resume.
   -

   VN: Driven by underperformance of XIXI (ADO MoM -18%). New distributors
   entered the market in July with a low-price strategy, disrupting pricing.
   The brand has since intervened to stabilize prices, and improvement is
   expected in August.

August Action Plan to Strengthen Beauty Program Performance:

   1.

   Expand the seller pool – We aim to onboard 74 additional shops (~11K ADO)
   starting in Aug, with a focus on high-potential beauty brands and some
   resellers. This diversification will help reduce reliance on individual
   brand sellers and mitigate performance risks when certain brands
   underperform.
   2.

   Closely monitor top sellers – Ensure timely identification of any
   operational issues especially during campaign periods.
   3.

   Upsize incentives for focus shops:
   -

      SG/MY: Incentive plan confirmed and rollout. (Double down on paid ads
      and rebates%)
      -

      TH/VN: Local teams to finalize incentive mechanisms by end of week.
      -

      PH: Progress slower due to non-participation in the previous phase.
      Regional PMO is in discussions with PH BD Head to improve
incentives which
      will help to attract more beauty sellers.

Despite July challenges, sellers in the Counter TB Beauty program
outperformed the overall Beauty category, with ADO growing +3% MoM vs -6%
for the category.


Slide 14

   -

   Interest amongst Beauty sellers is so low?

Lever 3 (ERP) has not ramped up yet, as Ju

*Closely monitor top sellers* – Ensure timely identification of any

content.answer:
JST's reluctance to mass collaborate directly, as they are concerned
   about reactions of other e-commerce partners, especially Taobao.

WDT’s limited mass-scale support, offering us only one livestream
   slot/mth. Last month's livestream gave us 11 converted sellers only.
   Nonetheless, this livestream will still be held.

Instead of mass pitching, we will focus on 1:1 outreach to WDT’s top 500
sellers, starting from today 11th Aug, together with internal Shopee
publicity channels and a PR article release. With this approach, we expect
to convert 392 additional sellers, which is significantly below our
original target of 7,763 sellers for August. However, each of these sellers
is expected to have a larger catalog than our original target (200 SKUs per
seller). We will also build a VIP onboarding channel for high-potential
sellers and evaluate the number of SKUs they can bring to Shopee. Detailed
timeline and plan below.

Slide 16

   -

   What exactly are we matching for here? Top TB vs Shopee? Or Top Shopee
   vs TB? Or something else?
   -

   Do not understand the color coding, why is negative green? Negative is
   supposed to be bad right?
   -

   There has not been much improvement wow?



We are currently matching the top TB items (1.8K SKUs) against Shopee CB
Shop to ensure that the most sold products on TBG are price competitive on
our platform.



The green color coding indicates categories that have shown WoW improvement
since we began co-sharing rebates with sellers on July 24. Going forward,
we will include an RR column in all future tables and apply the color
coding there instead.


For the 1.8K SKUs we are focusing on to improve price competitiveness in
July, we asked sellers to lower their listing prices by approximately 6% (a
tentative target, while the actual discount is being finalized using the BI
model). However, this reduction does not necessarily make all targeted SKUs
cheaper than TB in terms of total buyer-paid price. Some SKUs may still
remain more expensive overall if the total price gap exceeds 6%.

We aim to resolve thi

Hi Ian,

Lever 3 (ERP) has not ramped up yet, as July's seller outreach primarily
targeted traders through offline events where beauty sellers are not the
focus audience. Once mass pitching begins in August (Pending CN’s
confirmation), we expect Beauty to pick up, as it accounts for
approximately 40% of the ERP seller pool.

When exactly will we be able to start mass pitching?

Just received the latest update from ERP team. Mass pitching will not be
the focus of this month due to:

content.question: JST's reluctance to mass collaborate directly, as they are concerned
WDT’s limited mass-scale support, offering us only one livestream
JST's reluctance to mass collaborate directly, as they are concerned

content.answer:
JST's reluctance to mass collaborate directly, as they are concerned
   about reactions of other e-commerce partners, especially Taobao.

What changed? I thought they were ok to send messages to all sellers for us?

JST rejected the proposal, citing concerns that supporting our platform
could be perceived as favouring us. They fear that this may compromise
their platform neutrality and hinder broader business development efforts.
Alternatively, they agreed to provide us with seller leads from their city
managers, but the amount is small with just 40 sellers shared this month.
MKT team will be visiting these sellers 1:1 to pitch over this and next
month.

WDT agreed to a one-time push notification to its entire base, which MKT
team is preparing for now and aligning on format and ETA.

WDT’s limited mass-scale support, offering us only one livestream
   slot/mth. Last month's livestream gave us 11 converted sellers only.
   Nonetheless, this livestream will still be held.

How come so bad? How many viewers? What was the issue for the poor
conversion?

Please find conversion funnel for last month’s livestream below:

Conversion Stages

Stage 1:

Unique Seller Who Joined Live Stream

Stage 2:

Sellers Who Expressed Interest

Stage 3:

Seller Who Proceed With Onboarding

Stage 4:

Seller Who Completed Listing

#Sellers

1,775

44

11

2

Conversion %

100.0%

2.5%

0.6%

0.1%

Stage 1 > Stage 2:  Working with CN Marketing team to confirm the reason
for low conversion and generate more unique view - ETA: 15th Aug

Stage 2 > Stage 3: CN Marketing team has observed that sellers typically
require some time to reflect and internally align within their
organizations before deciding whether to proceed with onboarding.
Additionally, we have onboarded a new BPO team member to assist with seller
inquiries and to consistently follow up with reminders to prompt action on
a daily basis

Stage 3 > Stage 4: The main drop‑off stems from sellers not taking action
to list their SKUs, despite us successfully completing their onboarding
authorizations. We are working with the BPO team to follow up with these
sellers daily to prompt them to take action.


Instead of mass pitching, we will focus on 1:1 outreach to WDT’s top 500
sellers, starting from today 11th Aug, together with internal Shopee
publicity channels and a PR article release. With this approach, we expect
to convert 392 additional sellers, which is significantly below our
original target of 7,763 sellers for August. However, each of these sellers
is expected to have a larger catalog than our original target (200 SKUs per
seller). We will also build a VIP onboarding channel for high-potential
sellers and evaluate the number of SKUs they can

One-time push notification to all of WDT sellers that will happen this
   month. MKT team preparing the message and aligning on format + ETA with WDT

SAI aligned to start 1:1 pitching of 500 WDT sellers, and pitching has
   begun as of 13 Aug

++ @Ivy Chen <ivy.chen01@shopee.com> @Gerold Wang <gerold.wang@shopee.com>

Across 10 markets, we are at MTD 1K ADO. By EOM with the latest plan, we
estimate potentially landing at 2.6K ADO vs 10.2K ADO target.
I cannot agree current evaluation of 2.6k ado, we have too many
uncertainties, we are also too conservative and not deep enough to own each
channel's leads experience. @Nicholas Lim <nicholas.lim@shopee.com> and @Ivy
Chen <ivy.chen01@shopee.com> ,we need look at each step closely and improve
mass pitching channel. @Nicholas Lim <nicholas.lim@shopee.com>, for 1:1
pitching, we also need to be involved in the wording and process design
with Derek team, make sure the efficiency.

*Ian and Jianghong*
*Call out a help:* even we stretch above, don't think we can achieve 10k
ado target due to slowness of 聚水潭, Ivy and I had a few rounds catchup. Our
conclusion is:
JST is 暧昧 on commercial side (tech side is ok), they agreed on most of the
things to us, such as city level information, help us push etc, but just
very slow and much smaller than our expectation
As JST is 5X bigger seller pool of WDT, it is key for our success, see if
you can help push them?

Meanwhile, I heard marketing team and RM team are stretched on manual
resources, @Ivy Chen <ivy.chen01@shopee.com> and @Cheng Derek
<derek.cheng@shopee.com> you want to call out help? In general, I still
feel we are slow..

Thanks

Hi Shuning,

Thanks to Shuning's insights.

1) JST: So far our topline ADO target is on track. And we know that
leveraging JST sales is currently the most effective way to reach and
acquire key JST sellers. JST sales helps Shopee build trust with sellers
and will recommend highly motivated sellers to Shopee. Compared to
large-scale placement of JST internal banners or gaining exposure, JST
sales deliver better results and higher conversion rates. JST will help
recommend and visit key decision-makers among sellers, while those who see
the banners are usually just the operators of Taobao stores.

Therefore, JST has already selected three key cities for us that match the
Count taobao project seller cluster: Hangzhou, Shenzhen, and Foshan. Next
week, MKT will work with Cluster Sai to visit the sales leaders in these
cities one by one, and encourage the sales leaders to hold events or refer
sellers to Shopee. Three more key cities will be added next week.

2) WDT: WDT will promote the Swarm project to all WDT sellers this month
via pop-up messages in its ERP system.

And in August it is estimated that about 1,400 WDT seller leads will be
obtained.

Thanks a lot.

B.R

Ivy

Shuning Wang <shuning.wang@shopee.com> 于2025年8月14日周四 12:14写道：

content.answer:
@Shuning Wang <shuning.wang@shopee.com> is there a way to scrap for the
leads ourselves? Instead of relying on them?

BPM scraped top 1000 旺店通 sellers last week, we are working with marketing
team on communication plan to WDT and the scraped sellers, will review with
Jianghong next Monday. We are very cautious about this, afraid of breaking
relationships if WDT is aware we scraped them. Once aligned, I think this
is top priority to pitch for WDT.

For JST, we don't have a good way to scrape yet, they don't expose seller
info on website.



Instead of mass pitching, we will focus on 1:1 outreach to WDT’s top 500
sellers, starting from today 11th Aug, together with internal Shopee
publicity channels and a PR article release. With this approach, we expect
to convert 392 additional sellers, which is significantly below our
original target of 7,763 sellers for August. However, each of these sellers
is expected to have a larger catalog than our original target (200 SKUs per
seller). We will also build a VIP onboarding channel for high-potential
sellers and evaluate the number of SKUs they can bring to Shopee.



We should continue to do mass pitching regardless. JST will not do this for
us?

Jianghong correct me if wrong:

Sofar they disagreed to do mass pitching for us. They are ok to provide
city level seller info for us to pitch, but till now they only give us 3
cities' sales managers info, we need work through these 3 sales managers to
do pitching, very time consuming..



Where do we get the leads of the top 500 from? How can we do the same for
JST?

WDT had an offline workshop with their sellers last week, marketing team
screenshots the seller info. Sofar JST hasn't planned any offline workshop
with us.


Separately, we did 3 pr events ourselves in past a few days, 8.3k view, 0
leads being collected, sounds also not working.

content.answer:
Hi, Ian

非常感谢您的建议.

MKT and SMT will work with Shuning team to discuss how to set up this
incentive to effectively drive JST sales. At the same time, MKT will first
try to understand JST's expectations.

The direction for the incentive will be along these lines: From the local
market, incentives as SLS rebate on top of free commission period.

After discussing with Shuning, we will share the plan.


Thanks very much!!

B.R

Ivy
